heading: Special requirements for incentive auction of broadcast TV spectrum
label: 1452 Special requirements for incentive auction of broadcast TV spectrum
notes: Amendments2018—Subsecs. (j) to (n). added subsecs. (j) to (n).Pub. L. 115–141
amendments: Amendments2018—Subsecs. (j) to (n). added subsecs. (j) to (n).Pub. L. 115–141

children.heading: Reverse auction to identify incentive amount
children.label: a Reverse auction to identify incentive amount

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Commission shall conduct a reverse auction to determine the amount of compensation that each broadcast television licensee would accept in return for voluntarily relinquishing some or all of its broadcast television spectrum usage rights in order to make spectrum available for assignment through a system of competitive bidding under subparagraph (G) of .section 309(j)(8) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Relinquishing all usage rights with respect to a particular television channel without receiving in return any usage rights with respect to another television channel.

children.label: B
children.text: Relinquishing all usage rights with respect to an ultra high frequency television channel in return for receiving usage rights with respect to a very high frequency television channel.

children.label: C
children.text: Relinquishing usage rights in order to share a television channel with another licensee.

children.heading: Confidentiality
children.label: 3 Confidentiality
children.text: The Commission shall take all reasonable steps necessary to protect the confidentiality of Commission-held data of a licensee participating in the reverse auction under paragraph (1), including withholding the identity of such licensee until the reassignments and reallocations (if any) under subsection (b)(1)(B) become effective, as described in subsection (f)(2).

children.heading: Protection of carriage rights of licensees sharing a channel
children.label: 4 Protection of carriage rights of licensees sharing a channel
children.text: A broadcast television station that voluntarily relinquishes spectrum usage rights under this subsection in order to share a television channel and that possessed carriage rights under section 338, 534, or 535 of this title on , shall have, at its shared location, the carriage rights under such section that would apply to such station at such location if it were not sharing a channel.November 30, 2010

children.heading: Reorganization of broadcast TV spectrum
children.label: b Reorganization of broadcast TV spectrum
A
children.text: shall evaluate the broadcast television spectrum (including spectrum made available through the reverse auction under subsection (a)(1)); and

children.label: i
children.text: make such reassignments of television channels as the Commission considers appropriate; and

children.label: ii
children.text: reallocate such portions of such spectrum as the Commission determines are available for reallocation.

children.heading: Factors for consideration
children.label: 2 Factors for consideration
children.text: In making any reassignments or reallocations under paragraph (1)(B), the Commission shall make all reasonable efforts to preserve, as of , the coverage area and population served of each broadcast television licensee, as determined using the methodology described in OET Bulletin 69 of the Office of Engineering and Technology of the Commission.February 22, 2012

children.heading: No involuntary relocation from UHF to VHF
children.label: 3 No involuntary relocation from UHF to VHF
A
children.text: from an ultra high frequency television channel to a very high frequency television channel; or

children.label: B
children.text: from a television channel between the frequencies from 174 megahertz to 216 megahertz to a television channel between the frequencies from 54 megahertz to 88 megahertz.

children.label: i
children.text: a broadcast television licensee that was reassigned under paragraph (1)(B)(i) from one ultra high frequency television channel to a different ultra high frequency television channel, from one very high frequency television channel to a different very high frequency television channel, or, in accordance with subsection (g)(1)(B), from a very high frequency television channel to an ultra high frequency television channel, in order for the licensee to relocate its television service from one channel to the other;

children.label: I
children.text: is described in clause (i);

children.label: II
children.text: voluntarily relinquishes spectrum usage rights under subsection (a) with respect to an ultra high frequency television channel in return for receiving usage rights with respect to a very high frequency television channel; or

children.label: III
children.text: voluntarily relinquishes spectrum usage rights under subsection (a) to share a television channel with another licensee; or

children.label: iii
children.text: a channel 37 incumbent user, in order to relocate to other suitable spectrum, provided that all such users can be relocated and that the total relocation costs of such users do not exceed $300,000,000. For the purpose of this section, the spectrum made available through relocation of channel 37 incumbent users shall be deemed as spectrum reclaimed through a reverse auction under subsection (a).

children.heading: Regulatory relief
children.label: B Regulatory relief
children.text: In lieu of reimbursement for relocation costs under subparagraph (A), a broadcast television licensee may accept, and the Commission may grant as it considers appropriate, a waiver of the service rules of the Commission to permit the licensee, subject to interference protections, to make flexible use of the spectrum assigned to the licensee to provide services other than broadcast television services. Such waiver shall only remain in effect while the licensee provides at least 1 broadcast television program stream on such spectrum at no charge to the public.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: C Limitation
children.text: The Commission may not make reimbursements under subparagraph (A) for lost revenues.

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: D Deadline
children.text: The Commission shall make all reimbursements required by subparagraph (A) not later than the date that is 3 years after the completion of the forward auction under subsection (c)(1).

children.heading: Low-power television usage rights
children.label: 5 Low-power television usage rights
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to alter the spectrum usage rights of low-power television stations.

children.label: A
children.text: the Commission assigns licenses for the use of the spectrum that the Commission reallocates under subsection (b)(1)(B)(ii); and

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of the proceeds that the Commission shares under clause (i) of with each licensee whose bid the Commission accepts in the reverse auction under subsection (a)(1) is not less than the amount of such bid.section 309(j)(8)(G) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the amount of the proceeds from the forward auction under paragraph (1) is not greater than the sum described in subparagraph (B), no licenses shall be assigned through such forward auction, no reassignments or reallocations under subsection (b)(1)(B) shall become effective, and the Commission may not revoke any spectrum usage rights by reason of a bid that the Commission accepts in the reverse auction under subsection (a)(1).

children.label: i
children.text: the total amount of compensation that the Commission must pay successful bidders in the reverse auction under subsection (a)(1);

children.label: ii
children.text: the costs of conducting such forward auction that the salaries and expenses account of the Commission is required to retain under ; andsection 309(j)(8)(B) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: the estimated costs for which the Commission is required to make reimbursements under subsection (b)(4)(A).

children.heading: Administrative costs
children.label: C Administrative costs
children.text: The amount of the proceeds from the forward auction under paragraph (1) that the salaries and expenses account of the Commission is required to retain under shall be sufficient to cover the costs incurred by the Commission in conducting the reverse auction under subsection (a)(1), conducting the evaluation of the broadcast television spectrum under subparagraph (A) of subsection (b)(1), and making any reassignments or reallocations under subparagraph (B) of such subsection, in addition to the costs incurred by the Commission in conducting such forward auction.section 309(j)(8)(B) of this title

children.heading: Factor for consideration
children.label: 3 Factor for consideration
children.text: In conducting the forward auction under paragraph (1), the Commission shall consider assigning licenses that cover geographic areas of a variety of different sizes.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States a fund to be known as the TV Broadcaster Relocation Fund.

children.heading: Payment of relocation costs
children.label: 2 Payment of relocation costs
children.text: Any amounts borrowed under paragraph (3)(A) and any amounts in the TV Broadcaster Relocation Fund that are not necessary for reimbursement of the general fund of the Treasury for such borrowed amounts shall be available to the Commission to make the payments required by subsection (b)(4)(A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Beginning on the date when any reassignments or reallocations under subsection (b)(1)(B) become effective, as provided in subsection (f)(2), and ending when $1,000,000,000 has been deposited in the TV Broadcaster Relocation Fund, the Commission may borrow from the Treasury of the United States an amount not to exceed $1,000,000,000 to use toward the payments required by subsection (b)(4)(A).

children.heading: Reimbursement
children.label: B Reimbursement
children.text: The Commission shall reimburse the general fund of the Treasury, without interest, for any amounts borrowed under subparagraph (A) as funds are deposited into the TV Broadcaster Relocation Fund.

children.label: A
children.text: prior to the end of fiscal year 2022, transfer such amounts to the Public Safety Trust Fund established by ; andsection 1457(a)(1) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: after the end of fiscal year 2022, transfer such amounts to the general fund of the Treasury, where such amounts shall be dedicated for the sole purpose of deficit reduction.

children.heading: Numerical limitation on auctions and reorganization
children.label: e Numerical limitation on auctions and reorganization
children.text: The Commission may not complete more than one reverse auction under subsection (a)(1) or more than one reorganization of the broadcast television spectrum under subsection (b).

children.heading: Contemporaneous auctions and reorganization permitted
children.label: 1 Contemporaneous auctions and reorganization permitted
children.text: The Commission may conduct the reverse auction under subsection (a)(1), any reassignments or reallocations under subsection (b)(1)(B), and the forward auction under subsection (c)(1) on a contemporaneous basis.

children.heading: Effectiveness of reassignments and reallocations
children.label: 2 Effectiveness of reassignments and reallocations
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1), no reassignments or reallocations under subsection (b)(1)(B) shall become effective until the completion of the reverse auction under subsection (a)(1) and the forward auction under subsection (c)(1), and, to the extent practicable, all such reassignments and reallocations shall become effective simultaneously.

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: 3 Deadline
children.text: The Commission may not conduct the reverse auction under subsection (a)(1) or the forward auction under subsection (c)(1) after the end of fiscal year 2022.

children.heading: Limit on discretion regarding auction timing
children.label: 4 Limit on discretion regarding auction timing
children.text: shall not apply in the case of an auction conducted under this section.Section 309(j)(15)(A) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: in accordance with this section; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a violation by such licensee of the terms of its license or a specific provision of a statute administered by the Commission, or a regulation of the Commission promulgated under any such provision; or

children.label: i
children.text: such a reassignment will not decrease the total amount of ultra high frequency spectrum made available for reallocation under this section; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a request from such licensee for the reassignment was pending at the Commission on .May 31, 2011

children.label: A
children.text: the first date when the reverse auction under subsection (a)(1), the reassignments and reallocations (if any) under subsection (b)(1)(B), and the forward auction under subsection (c)(1) have been completed;

children.label: B
children.text: the date of a determination by the Commission that the amount of the proceeds from the forward auction under subsection (c)(1) is not greater than the sum described in subsection (c)(2)(B); or

children.label: C
children.text: .September 30, 2022

children.heading: Protest right inapplicable
children.label: h Protest right inapplicable
children.text: The right of a licensee to protest a proposed order of modification of its license under shall not apply in the case of a modification made under this section.section 316 of this title
expand or contract the authority of the Commission, except as otherwise expressly provided; or

prevent the implementation of the Commission’s “White Spaces” Second Report and Order and Memorandum Opinion and Order (FCC 08–260, adopted ) in the spectrum that remains allocated for broadcast television use after the reorganization required by such subsection.November 4, 2008

children.heading: Reserve source for payment of relocation costs
children.label: j Reserve source for payment of relocation costs
A
children.text: for fiscal year 2018, $600,000,000, to remain available, notwithstanding subsection (d)(4), until not later than , pursuant to this subsection; andJuly 3, 2023

children.label: B
children.text: for fiscal year 2019, $400,000,000, to remain available, notwithstanding subsection (d)(4), until not later than , pursuant to this subsection.July 3, 2023

children.label: i
children.text: reimbursements pursuant to subsection (b)(4)(A)(i) or (b)(4)(A)(ii), including not more than $350,000,000 for this purpose from funds made available by paragraph (1)(A);

children.label: ii
children.text: payments required by subsection (k), including not more than $150,000,000 for this purpose from funds made available by paragraph (1)(A);

children.label: iii
children.text: payments required by subsection (l), including not more than $50,000,000 for this purpose from funds made available by paragraph (1)(A); and

children.label: iv
children.text: payments solely for the purposes of consumer education relating to the reorganization of broadcast television spectrum under subsection (b), including $50,000,000 for this purpose from funds made available by paragraph (1)(A).

children.heading: Certification
children.label: B Certification
children.text: The certification described in this subparagraph is a certification from the Commission to the Secretary of the Treasury that the funds available prior to , in the TV Broadcaster Relocation Fund are likely to be insufficient to reimburse reasonably incurred costs described in subsection (b)(4)(A)(i) or (b)(4)(A)(ii).March 23, 2018

children.heading: For payments to broadcast television licensees and MVPDs
children.label: i For payments to broadcast television licensees and MVPDs
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (b)(4)(D), the Commission may make payments pursuant to subsection (b)(4)(A)(i) or (b)(4)(A)(ii) from amounts made available to the TV Broadcaster Relocation Fund by paragraph (1) after , if, before making any such payments after such date, the Commission submits to Congress a certification that such payments are necessary to reimburse reasonably incurred costs described in such subsection.April 13, 2020

children.heading: For payments to television translator stations and low power television stations
children.label: ii For payments to television translator stations and low power television stations
children.text: Amounts made available to the TV Broadcaster Relocation Fund by paragraph (1) shall not be available to the Commission to make payments required by subsection (k) after , unless, before making any such payments after such date, the Commission submits to Congress a certification that such payments are necessary to reimburse costs reasonably incurred by a television translator station or low power television station (as such terms are defined in subsection (k)) on or after , in order for such station to relocate its television service from one channel to another channel or otherwise modify its facility as a result of the reorganization of broadcast television spectrum under subsection (b).April 13, 2020January 1, 2017

children.heading: For payments to FM broadcast stations
children.label: iii For payments to FM broadcast stations
children.text: Amounts made available to the TV Broadcaster Relocation Fund by paragraph (1) shall not be available to the Commission to make payments required by subsection (l) after , unless, before making any such payments after such date, the Commission submits to Congress a certification that such payments are necessary to reimburse costs reasonably incurred by an FM broadcast station (as defined in subsection (l)) for facilities necessary for such station to reasonably minimize disruption of service as a result of the reorganization of broadcast television spectrum under subsection (b).April 13, 2020

children.heading: Unused funds rescinded and deposited into the general fund of the treasury
children.label: 3 Unused funds rescinded and deposited into the general fund of the treasury

children.heading: Rescission and deposit
children.label: A Rescission and deposit
children.text: If any unobligated amounts made available to the TV Broadcaster Relocation Fund by paragraph (1) remain in the Fund after the date described in subparagraph (B), such amounts shall be rescinded and deposited into the general fund of the Treasury, where such amounts shall be dedicated for the sole purpose of deficit reduction.

children.label: i
children.text: the date of a certification by the Commission under subparagraph (C) that all reimbursements pursuant to subsections (b)(4)(A)(i) and (b)(4)(A)(ii) have been made and that all reimbursements pursuant to subsections (k) and (l) have been made; or

children.heading: Certification
children.label: C Certification
children.text: If all reimbursements pursuant to subsections (b)(4)(A)(i) and (b)(4)(A)(ii) and all reimbursements pursuant to subsections (k) and (l) have been made before , the Commission shall submit to the Secretary of the Treasury a certification that all such reimbursements have been made.July 3, 2023

children.heading: Administrative costs
children.label: 4 Administrative costs
children.text: The amount of auction proceeds that the salaries and expenses account of the Commission is required to retain under , including from the proceeds of the forward auction under this section, shall be sufficient to cover the administrative costs incurred by the Commission in making any reimbursements out of the TV Broadcaster Relocation Fund from amounts made available to that Fund by paragraph (1).section 309(j)(8)(B) of this title

children.heading: Payment of relocation costs of television translator stations and low power television stations
children.label: k Payment of relocation costs of television translator stations and low power television stations

children.heading: Payment required
children.label: 1 Payment required
children.text: From amounts made available under subsection (j)(2), the Commission shall reimburse costs reasonably incurred by a television translator station or low power television station on or after , in order for such station to relocate its television service from one channel to another channel or otherwise modify its facility as a result of the reorganization of broadcast television spectrum under subsection (b). Only stations that are eligible to file and do file an application in the Commission’s Special Displacement Window are eligible to seek reimbursement under this paragraph.January 1, 2017

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: The Commission may not make reimbursements under paragraph (1) for lost revenues.

children.label: A
children.text: if the licensee of such station has received reimbursement with respect to such station under subsection (b)(4)(A)(i) (including from amounts made available under subsection (j)(2)(A)(i)), or from any other source, such station may not receive reimbursement under paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: if such station has received reimbursement under paragraph (1), the licensee of such station may not receive reimbursement with respect to such station under subsection (b)(4)(A)(i).

children.heading: Additional limitation
children.label: 4 Additional limitation
children.text: The Commission may not make reimbursement under paragraph (1) for costs incurred to resolve mutually exclusive applications, including costs incurred in any auction of available channels.

children.heading: Low power television station
children.label: A Low power television station
children.text: The term “low power television station” means a low power TV station (as defined in section 74.701 of title 47, Code of Federal Regulations) that was licensed and transmitting for at least 9 of the 12 months prior to . For purposes of the preceding sentence, the operation of analog and digital companion facilities may be combined.April 13, 2017

children.heading: Television translator station
children.label: B Television translator station
children.text: The term “television translator station” means a television broadcast translator station (as defined in section 74.701 of title 47, Code of Federal Regulations) that was licensed and transmitting for at least 9 of the 12 months prior to . For purposes of the preceding sentence, the operation of analog and digital companion facilities may be combined.April 13, 2017

children.heading: Payment of Relocation Costs of FM Broadcast Stations
children.label: l Payment of Relocation Costs of FM Broadcast Stations

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: From amounts made available under subsection (j)(2), the Commission shall reimburse costs reasonably incurred by an FM broadcast station for facilities necessary for such station to reasonably minimize disruption of service as a result of the reorganization of broadcast television spectrum under subsection (b).

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The Commission may not make reimbursements under subparagraph (A) for lost revenues.

children.heading: Duplicative payments prohibited
children.label: C Duplicative payments prohibited
children.text: If an FM broadcast station has received a payment for interim facilities from the licensee of a television broadcast station that was reimbursed for such payment under subsection (b)(4)(A)(i) (including from amounts made available under subsection (j)(2)(A)(i)), or from any other source, such FM broadcast station may not receive any reimbursements under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: FM broadcast station defined
children.label: 2 FM broadcast station defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term ‘FM broadcast station’ has the meaning given such term in section 73.310 of title 47, Code of Federal Regulations, and includes an FM translator, which has the meaning given the term “FM translator” in section 74.1201 of such title.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Commission shall complete a rulemaking to implement subsections (k) and (l).March 23, 2018

children.heading: Matters for inclusion
children.label: 2 Matters for inclusion
children.text: The rulemaking completed under paragraph (1) shall include the development of lists of reasonable eligible costs to be reimbursed by the Commission pursuant to subsections (k) and (l), and procedures for the submission and review of cost estimates and other materials related to those costs consistent with the regulations developed by the Commission pursuant to subsection (b)(4).
Nothing in subsections (j) through (m) shall alter the final transition phase completion date established by the Commission for full power and Class A television stations.
